title: generate_eui64() leaks SHA-256 state on error paths
description:
In lora_l2.c generate_eui64(), the tc_sha256_state_struct is stack-allocated (line 98) and contains internal hash state. If tc_sha256_init, tc_sha256_update, or tc_sha256_final fails (lines 101-112), the function returns early without zeroing sha_state.

Trigger: SHA-256 library failure (rare but possible on constrained devices or library misconfiguration).

Bad behavior: Stack frame containing partial SHA state remains until overwritten by subsequent function calls. While the entropy leakage is limited (only affects the hwid hash, not long-term secrets), it violates defense-in-depth: sensitive cryptographic state should be zeroed on all exit paths.

Note: hash[] buffer IS zeroed on success (line 119), but neither hash[] nor sha_state are zeroed on error.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c lines 98-112
